steps.title: Recognize complementary angles in the right triangle
steps.content: In right triangle RST, we're told that the sum of angles R and S equals 90°. This means R and S are complementary angles - together they make a right angle.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity
steps.content:
Since $R + S = 90°$, we can write $S = 90° - R$. Therefore:
$$\cos(S) = \cos(90° - R) = \sin(R)$$

steps.title: Substitute the given value and identify the answer
steps.content:
We're given that $\sin(R) = \frac{\sqrt{17}}{13}$.
By the cofunction identity from Step 2: $$\cos(S) = \sin(R) = \frac{\sqrt{17}}{13}$$ That matches choice B - B is correct.
